reasoning: By November 2025, temperature data for the first ten months of the year is already known. The transition to a La Niña pattern in late 2024/2025 generally leads to lower global mean temperatures compared to the strong El Niño conditions of 2023-2024. The Polymarket price of 2% in late October 2025 reflects this reality: 2025 is clearly tracking well below the record temperatures set in 2023 and 2024. With only two months left in the year, it is practically impossible for 2025 to make up the deficit and become the hottest year on record.
